title.description: Product or concept name. Used as hero title and filename slug.

body.description: Concept description, any length. The skill detects input type and adapts.

prior_analysis.behavior: Extract insights, translate to product language, map to sections

existing_brief.behavior: Parse existing structure, identify what to update

examples: A privacy-first legal research tool for everyday Americans. Post-Heppner, consumer AI chats are discoverable in court. 80 million Americans face civil legal issues yearly but can't afford lawyers. We build a legal research environment with architectural zero data retention — self-hosted inference, volatile memory only, no logging layer.
[Full GMR Navigation Block 7 output pasted here]
Competitor: DoNotPay ($9.99/mo). Competitor: AI Lawyer ($5.99/week). Gap: none offer architectural privacy guarantees.

domain.description: Industry/market. Propagates to expert panel system prompts.
domain.propagation: Block 4 expert prompts receive domain context

audience.description: Primary reader. Controls vocabulary depth and metrics emphasis.

calibration.investors: Focus on market size, moat, returns. Lighter on technical depth.
calibration.team: Focus on feasibility, architecture, timeline. Full technical depth.
calibration.partners: Focus on integration, compatibility, mutual value.
calibration.board: Focus on risk, strategy, competitive position. Executive summary style.

calibration.concept: Market validation focus. Lighter metrics. Pricing hypotheses. Technical feasibility questions.
calibration.mvp: Early traction focus. Customer acquisition data. Technical architecture decisions. First revenue signals.
calibration.growth: Unit economics focus. LTV/CAC. Scaling challenges. Competitive dynamics. Margin analysis.

analysis_id.description: ID for prior deep analysis. Skill reads analysis data and maps findings into brief structure.

mapping.central_finding: Maps to WHY NOW or HOW IT WORKS as strategic insight
mapping.blind_spots: Maps to RISKS & SCENARIOS as identified risks
mapping.thematic_groups: Shapes section emphasis and content weighting
mapping.supporting_concepts: Become analogies and positioning devices in THE SOLUTION

constraints.description: Hard constraints that shape the brief.
examples: Budget: $50K for MVP. Timeline: 8 weeks. Team: 2 engineers.
Regulatory: must operate within TRAIGA sandbox. No external API dependencies.
Geographic: Texas only at launch. Must not cross UPL boundary.

prior_art.description: Known competitors. Seeds Block 1 competitive research.
examples: DoNotPay, AI Lawyer, Courtroom5, Rocket Lawyer

version.description: Brief version number. Version > 1 loads previous version and tracks delta.
version.delta_behavior: Block 1 focuses on what changed. Block 5 adds 'Changes from v{N-1}' section.
